comments.body: I'm not sure if the errors are caused by the Ruff update. Anyway, what do you think about enabling linters to run in a GitHub Action? I think it would be faster than running everything in Packit. I can set it up.

> I'm not sure if the errors are caused by the Ruff update.

the errors are fixed in 567

> Anyway, what do you think about enabling linters to run in a GitHub Action? I think it would be faster than running everything in Packit. I can set it up.

linters are already in a github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/.github/workflows/pre-commit.yml run by pre-commit. the packit action runs pre-commit on the whole codebase in a tox environment.

> > Anyway, what do you think about enabling linters to run in a GitHub Action? I think it would be faster than running everything in Packit. I can set it up.
> 
> linters are already in a github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/.github/workflows/pre-commit.yml run by pre-commit. the packit action runs pre-commit on the whole codebase in a tox environment.

Thanks for the explanation.

I just did a quick check and a lot of ruff issues were fixed with normal linting with time, so I removed a bunch of them and fixed the few issues in the code.

There's one to not in the tests which was a mistyped `tearDown` function, it was called `tearUp`. The linter caught it right away but it was there since 2021. I decided to include it in this linting PR + ruff upgrade

@Honny1: changing LGTM is restricted to collaborators
